titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.18

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=644 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:30:27.695953+00 | translation moa=645 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:30:25.769453+00

p.cl: Willelmus dei gratia Rex scottorum . omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue salutem .

me presente . et presentibus episcopis .
baronibus . et pluribus aliis probis hominibus Regni mei . die Sancti Luce Euuangeliste . inter monachos de Meylros et homines de Wedal’
super pastura foreste mee quam antecessores mei et ego . dictis monachis de Meylros in puram et perpetuam elemosinam
dedimus . per iuramenta fidelium uirorum facta fuit perambulacio et certa finium limitacio . Iurauerunt siquidem coram me et
probis hominibus meis predicti fideles . quod foresta mea que est pastura dictorum monachorum de Meylros . tendit usque ad
uiam que uadit ad occidentem parte ecclesie de Wedale et usque ad Riuulum qui uocatur fasseburne .

Quare uolo .
concedo et precipio . quod dicti monachi totam prefatam pasturam cum omnibus aisiamentis et libertatibus suis . pacifice teneant et possideant . usque ad prefatas diuisas . in puram . liberam et perpetuam elemosinam . proprie sibi et suis successoribus . Ita quod
nulli communiam habeant inter ipsos aut cum ipsis infra prefatas diuisas predicte pasture . saluis mihi et heredibus
meis . feris et auibus et uenacione infra dictam forestam .

Test’ Ricardo Episcopo Sancti Andree . Iocelino Episcopo Glasg’.
Iohanne de kalchou . Osberto abbate de Iedword’ . Comite . dauid . Ricado de Moruyll’ constabulario et multis aliis .
William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land [sends] greeting.

in my presence, and with bishops, barons, and many other worthy men of my realm being present, on the Day of Saint Luke the Evangelist, by the oaths of sworn men, perambulation was made and the limitation of the boundaries fixed between the monks of Melrose and the men of Wedale concerning the pasture of my forest that my ancestors and I gave to the said monks of Melrose in pure and everlasting alms. The aforesaid sworn men indeed swore an oath in front of me and my worthy men, that my forest, which is the pasture of the aforesaid monks of Melrose, extends to the road that leads to the western side of the church of Wedale and as far as the stream which is called Fasseburn.

Wherefore I will, grant and instruct that the said monks peaceably hold and possess the whole pasture aforementioned with all its easements and liberties as far as the boundaries aforementioned in pure, free and everlasting alms, as their own property and their successors', so that none shall have common between them or with them within the aforementioned boundaries of the said pasture, saving to myself and my heirs wild beasts, birds and game within the said forest.
As witnesses Richard bishop of St Andrews, Jocelin bishop of Glasgow, John of Kelso, Osbert abbot of Jedburgh, Earl David, Richard de Morville the constable and many others.
